target.name: Clippership
target.founder: Nico Cymbalist, Luca Cymbalist, Kai Matsuka

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Clippership is a Seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Nico Cymbalist, Luca Cymbalist, and Kai Matsuka. The company has raised $5M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of three private peers in the ocean and maritime sector—Standard Subsea, NetworkOcean, and Thalassa Robotics—with no public market comparables available in this space.

Valuation analysis applies a stage-progression methodology, the primary applicable approach given limited public benchmarks and early-stage status. Using the $5M total raised as a proxy for base value, a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $12M. This reflects the expected value accretion between capital raises at this stage, absent material revenue or profitability data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology; robust triangulation would require additional comp-based or discounted cash flow approaches, constrained here by the absence of public peers and likely limited financial disclosure from private comparables. Key uncertainties include the composition and financial metrics of the three private peers, product-market fit validation, and the addressable market size within ocean and maritime technology. Additional data on peer funding rounds and growth trajectories would strengthen confidence in this range.
